name: Alec Pierce
headline: Field-stretcher inherits WR1 role, but the profile screams boom-bust
body:
Pierce broke through in 2025: 1,003 yards on just 47 grabs, a 21.3-yard average, 6 scores and a WR19 advanced finish. The efficiency is real and rare, a 0.664 EPA per play, 53.6% success rate, 2.22 yards per route and a 36.4% air-yards share that ranked him among the league's premier vertical threats. His hands held up too, one drop on 48 catchable balls. The catch: it was built on an 18.96 air-yards-per-target diet, the most volatile way to score fantasy points.

With Pittman gone, he's the nominal top target, yet the role demands underneath work he's never logged, and Tyler Warren plus Josh Downs will eat short-area volume. A 15.8% target share and 56% catch rate cap the floor, and his ceiling rides on Daniel Jones throwing deep. An offseason ankle procedure may cost early camp reps. Treat him as a sixth-round dart with WR2 upside and weekly variance.
